_source: connectedhomeip @5cd2917 src/crypto/tests/TestChipCryptoPAL.cpp (TestGroup_SessionIdDerivation, kGroupSessionId1/2) + src/transport/raw/tests/TestPeerAddress.cpp (TestPeerAddressMulticast, hardcoded `expected[]`) — INDEPENDENT known-answer oracle. Group-session-id KATs additionally reproduced byte-for-byte by a separate Python3 hmac/hashlib HKDF-SHA256 (no Rust involved); see _independence_check.
_session_id_kdf: session_id = BigEndian::Get16( HKDF-SHA256(ikm=operational_group_key, salt=[] (zero-length), info="GroupKeyHash", L=2) ). NOTE: the info string is the 12-byte ASCII "GroupKeyHash" (47726f75704b657948617368) — NOT "GroupKeyHash v1.0"; chip's source comment says v1.0 but the actual kGroupKeyHashInfo[] byte array carries no " v1.0" suffix. Bit rule: take the first 2 output bytes, interpret big-endian as the u16 (out[0]<<8 | out[1]). No masking / no bit-set rule applied.
_multicast_layout: 16-byte IPv6 (ff35::/...): [0]=0xff,[1]=0x35 (multicast, flags=3 => non-perm/has-prefix, scope=5 site-local); [2]=0x00,[3]=0x40 (prefix length 0x40=64); [4]=0xfd (locally-assigned ULA designator); [5..11]=upper 56 bits of FabricId in BIG-ENDIAN ((fabric>>8)&0x00ffffffffffffff); [12]=lowest 8 bits of FabricId (fabric & 0xff); [13]=0x00; [14..15]=GroupId in BIG-ENDIAN. Group-id endianness = big-endian (network byte order).

group_session_id_vectors._note: chip self-consistent KAT #1: kGroupOperationalKey1 -> kGroupSessionId1. Both hardcoded in TestChipCryptoPAL.cpp.
chip self-consistent KAT #2: kGroupOperationalKey2 -> kGroupSessionId2. Both hardcoded in TestChipCryptoPAL.cpp.

operational_group_key_vectors._note: chip TestGroup_OperationalKeyDerivation 'Epoch Key 3 (example from spec)': kEpochKeyBuffer3 + kCompressedFabricId2 -> kGroupOperationalKey3. Independent chip KAT for the op-key step (Salt=CFID, Info="GroupKey v1.0", L=16).

multicast_ipv6_vectors._note: chip TestPeerAddressMulticast hardcoded expected[] for BuildMatterPerGroupMulticastAddress(fabric, group).

_anchor_chain._note: Relationship to the already-validated operational_ipk_matches_matter_js test in crates/matter-crypto/src/operational.rs. That test's anchor uses epoch_key 235bf7e62823d358dcf746bfa7541cf2 (DIFFERENT from chip's kEpochKeyBuffer3 above, which is 235bf7e62823d358dca4ba50b1535f4b) with cfid 87e1b004e235a130 -> op_group_key daee424f434677afb5949706572b4ccb. The group_session_id below was computed FROM that anchor op key by the SAME independent Python HKDF that reproduced chip's KATs (so the value is independently derived, not self-referential vs the future Rust formula).

_independence_check.tool: python3 hmac+hashlib HKDF-SHA256 (one-off script, no Rust)
_independence_check.command: python3 derive: prk=HMAC-SHA256(key=0x00*32, msg=op_key); okm=HKDF-expand(prk, info="GroupKeyHash", L=2); sid=(okm[0]<<8)|okm[1]
_independence_check.result: reproduced chip's hardcoded kGroupSessionId1=0x6c80 and kGroupSessionId2=0x0c48 exactly, confirming the KDF parameters (empty salt, info="GroupKeyHash", L=2, big-endian Get16).
